categories.title: Obligation of Fasting
categories.description: The foundational verses establishing fasting as an obligation for believers, including the command and its connection to taqwa.

verses.relevanceNote: The primary verse prescribing fasting for believers, connecting it to previous nations and emphasizing taqwa as its purpose.
Specifies fasting is for a counted number of days, with provisions for illness and travel.
Identifies Ramadan as the month of fasting, the month the Quran was revealed, emphasizing guidance and criterion.

categories.title: Exemptions & Provisions
categories.description: Verses detailing who is exempt from fasting and the provisions for travelers, the sick, and those facing hardship.

verses.relevanceNote: Details exemptions: illness, travel, and the option for those with difficulty to feed the poor instead.
Allah intends ease not hardship - the principle underlying all exemptions.
Allah does not burden a soul beyond its capacity - foundational principle for fasting exemptions.
Context on conditions that temporarily invalidate worship states, relevant to fasting purification.

categories.title: Spiritual Benefits
categories.description: Verses highlighting the spiritual rewards, connection to taqwa, and the blessed nature of fasting and the month of Ramadan.

verses.relevanceNote: Fasting is prescribed so that you may attain taqwa (God-consciousness) - the primary spiritual benefit.
Laylatul Qadr (Night of Power) - the blessed night in Ramadan better than a thousand months.
What will make you know what the Night of Power is?
The Night of Power is better than a thousand months of worship.
Angels and the Spirit descend on this blessed night with every decree.
Peace it is until the emergence of dawn - describing the nature of this blessed night.
Allah is near and responds to supplication - emphasizing closeness to Allah during fasting.

categories.title: Historical Context
categories.description: Verses showing fasting was prescribed for previous nations and prophets, establishing its universal spiritual significance.

verses.relevanceNote: Fasting prescribed for you as it was prescribed for those before you - showing continuity with previous nations.
Maryam (Mary) was commanded to fast from speech - showing fasting takes different forms.
Regulations for Ramadan fasting including permitted activities during the night, abrogating earlier stricter rules.
Fasting three days as expiation for broken oaths - showing fasting as spiritual compensation.
Fasting two consecutive months as expiation - another instance of fasting as spiritual remedy.
